assertions.description: Origin-keyed process isolation only meaningfully fires for HTTPS origins; the page must be in a secure context.
window.originAgentCluster is a boolean reflecting whether the document is in an origin-keyed agent cluster — the introspection point for origin isolation.
window.crossOriginIsolated is the related introspection bit for COOP/COEP isolation; it remains a boolean.
location.origin returns the page's origin string, the unit process isolation is keyed on.
document.domain is still present as a writable IDL attribute; origin-keyed agent clusters disable its setter, but the property surface remains.
Assigning document.domain in an origin-keyed agent cluster either throws or leaves the origin unit unchanged.
